content.full_text:
June 18, 1976

Ms. Linda Muir  
Office of the Lieutenant Governor  
Room 1028, State Capitol  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Muir:

The purpose of this letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on June 16, 1976.

You presented the following facts: An advisory committee to Lieutenant Governor Dymally was formed in San Francisco a few months ago by a group of businessmen. The Lieutenant Governor and other state officials speak at their meetings. An amount of money was collected from each member to cover the costs of lunches and the publication of a newsletter. Subsequently, the group decided to make a $500 contribution to Lieutenant Governor Dymally. You asked what, if any, filing obligations the group has under the Political Reform Act.

I advised that if, at the time the collection was made, no portion of the funds were earmarked for political purposes, the group will become a committee, as defined in Government Code Sections 82013(b) or 82013(c), subject to filing requirements, once its expenditures in behalf of candidates or committees amount to $500 or more in a calendar year or once its monetary contributions directly to candidates and committees total $5,000 or more in a calendar year. I further stated that if the group begins collecting funds for political purposes, it will become a committee under Government Code Section 82013(a) once it has collected $500 or more in a calendar year.

Enclosed are copies of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18215 and 18225 which define "political purpose," "contribution" and "expenditure." If you have further questions, please contact our staff.

Sincerely,

NAN HAMBLETON  
Special Compliance Representative

NH:mak  
Enclosures   bcc: Jeanne-A
